question: A collection of instructions that performs a single logical function is called ____________
A terminated transaction that has completed its execution successfully is ____________ otherwise it is __________
The state of the data accessed by an aborted transaction must be restored to what it was just before the transaction started executing. This restoration is known as ________ of transaction.
Write ahead logging is a way ____________

options.c: that records data on stable storage

question: In the write ahead logging a _____________ is maintained.
An actual update is not allowed to a data item ____________

options.a: before the corresponding log record is written out to stable storage
options.b: after the corresponding log record is written out to stable storage
options.c: until the whole log record has been checked for inconsistencies

question: The undo and redo operations must be _________ to guarantee correct behaviour, even if a failure occurs during recovery process.
explanation: Idempotent – Multiple executions of an operation have the same result as does one execution.

question: The system periodically performs checkpoints that consists of the following operation(s) ____________

options.a: Putting all the log records currently in main memory onto stable storage
options.b: putting all modified data residing in main memory onto stable storage
options.c: putting a log record onto stable storage

question: Consider a transaction T1 that committed prior to checkpoint. The <T1 commits> record appears in the log before the <checkpoint> record. Any modifications made by T1 must have been written to the stable storage either with the checkpoint or prior to it. Thus at recovery time ____________

options.a: There is a need to perform an undo operation on T1
options.b: There is a need to perform a redo operation on T1
options.c: There is no need to perform an undo and redo operation on T1

options.a: concurrent execution of transactions is equivalent to the transactions executed serially
options.b: the transactions can be carried out one after the other
options.c: a valid result occurs after execution transactions

question: A locking protocol is one that ____________

options.c: governs how locks are acquired and released

question: The two phase locking protocol consists of ____________
The growing phase is a phase in which?

options.a: A transaction may obtain locks, but does not release any
options.b: A transaction may obtain locks, and releases a few or all of them
options.c: A transaction may release locks, but does not obtain any new locks
options.d: A transaction may release locks, and does obtain new locks

question: The shrinking phase is a phase in which?

options.a: A transaction may obtain locks, but does not release any
options.b: A transaction may obtain locks, and releases a few or all of them
options.c: A transaction may release locks, but does not obtain any new locks
options.d: A transaction may release locks, and does obtain new locks

question: Which of the following concurrency control protocols ensure both conflict serializability and freedom from deadlock?
